rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Contestants Motion For Reconsideration Of Contestants November 18, 2022 Evidentiary Hearing Memorandum In Support Declaration Of Peter Bresler Esq And Proposed Order (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 3] The evidentiary hearing was held as scheduled, on November 18, 2022. Contrary to the Order Setting Trial Re Adoption and Related Orders' instruction that "the interpreter and the parties who will rely on the interpreter's services must be in [sic] in-person in the courtroom," Yu appeared in the courtroom, needing the services of an interpreter, while the interpreter appeared remotely via Zoom. The court worked to configure the courtroom and the courtroom's technology, to allow for the hearing to proceed in this manner, with remote interpretation services. (Bresler Decl. at paragraph 4.) Ultimately, however, an in-person interpreter showed up and provided interpreter services to Yu. The court heard argument and evidence. At the close of the hearing, the court granted leave to the parties to submit supplemental trial briefing, including briefing on the applicability of Probate Code section 6451 to the facts at issue in the case; whether equitable adoption severs the adoptees' relationship with their biological family; and whether an adult adoption can be completed via equitable adoption. On November 28, 2022, Yu filed the instant Motion for Reconsideration. On November 30, 2022, Yu and Kmucha filed their separate Supplemental Trial Briefs, as directed to do so by the court at the November 18, 2022 hearing. On December 9, 2022, the court entered Order After Hearing Re Adoption; Setting Hearing on the Petitions; and Directing Service of This Order. The court found that Jack Genz had adopted Lambert Genz. On December 28, 2022, Kmucha filed Objection to Contestant's Motion for Reconsideration of Contestant's Nov 18, 2022 Evidentiary Hearing. On January 11,2023, Yu filed Contestant's Declaration in Support of Petition for Rehearing on Issue of the Effect of Probate Code 6451. Yu's motion for reconsideration under Code of Civil Procedure section 1008(a) is DENIED. Section 1008(a) provides: When an application for an order has been made to a judge, or to a court, and refused in whole or in part, or granted, or granted conditionally, or on terms, any party affected by the order may, within 10 days after service upon the party of written notice of entry of the order and based upon new or different facts, circumstances, or law, make application to the same judge or court that made the order, to reconsider the matter and modify, amend, or revoke the prior order. The party making the application shall state by affidavit what application was made before, when and to what judge, what order or decisions were made, and what new or different facts, circumstances, or law are claimed to be shown. The motion is procedurally defective. Section 1008(a) allows "any party affected by the order [] [to] make application to the same judge or court that made the order, to reconsider the matter and modify, amend, or revoke the prior order." Yu filed her motion on November 28, 2022-before the court issued its order (and before the matter was even submitted). [Part 2 of 3]

rulings.Calendar Matter: Contestants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Contestants November 18, 2022 Evidentiary Hearing Memorandum In Support Declaration Of Peter Bresler Esq And Proposed Order
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 3] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. On August 24, 2022, two competing petitions for letters of administration in this matter were heard. Petitioner Yu requested an evidentiary hearing on the issue whether Jack Genz adopted Lambert Genz. The court granted the request and set the evidentiary hearing for November 18. Subsequently, the court issued an Order Setting Trial Re Adoption and Related Orders. The Order stated, in part, the following, under the heading "Trial on Issue of Adoption:" Petitioner Kmucha contends that John "Jack" Gen[z] adopted decedent Lambert Genz and, if true, Kmucha has priority for appointment as the representation of decedent's estate. Thus, the adoption issue is material to resolution of these petitions. Petitioner Yu disputes Kmucha's contention. The hearing on the competing petitions for letters of administration is continued to November 18, 2022, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204 of the above-entitled court, at 400 McAllister Street, San Francisco, CA. At the same time, date, and place, the court will hear evidence that will allow it to decide whether or not John "Jack" Genz adopted decedent Lambert Genz. Kmucha bears the burden of proof on the adoption issue, and he will present his evidence first and have an opportunity to rebut Yu's evidentiary presentation. Kmucha and Yu will each have up to 1.5 hours to present evidence and argue their positions. Any party wishing to be heard at trial must file a brief setting forth their position on the adoption issue and the facts and law that support their position on the adoption issue and the facts and law that support their position. Any such brief must be filed no later than November 4, 2022, and courtesy copies must be delivered to the court no later than 4:00 p.m. on the same day. If any party of witness desires the services of an interpreter, counsel for the party or for the party who will be calling the witness desiring interpreter services must follow the court's request procedure, which is set out at http://www/sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/interpreters. Regardless of the parties' stipulation regarding a remote trial proceeding and regardless of whether the services of a court or private interpreter are used, the interpreter and the parties who will rely on the interpreter's services must be in [sic] in-person in the courtroom. Order Setting Trial Re Adoption and Related Orders at 2:3-27. As allowed by the court in the Order Setting Trial Re Adoption and Related Orders, the parties stipulated to conducting the November 18, 2022 evidentiary hearing remotely via Zoom. (Stipulation and Order entered November 2, 2022.) On November 4, 2022, Yu filed (1) Trial Brief on Evidentiary Hearing on Issue of Whether Lambert Genz Was Adopted; and (2) Exhibit 1 to 21 to Evidentiary Hearing on Issue of Whether Lambert Genz Was Adopted. On November 7, 2022, Yu filed Supplemental Trial Brief on Evidentiary Hearing on Issue of Whether Lambert Genz Was Adopted. On November 14, 2022, Yu filed Second Supplemental Trial Brief on Evidentiary Hearing on Issue of Whether Lambert Genz Was Adopted. [Part 1 of 3]

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Contestants Motion For Reconsideration Of Contestants November 18, 2022 Evidentiary Hearing Memorandum In Support Declaration Of Peter Bresler Esq And Proposed Order (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 3] Even if Yu had brought her motion after the December 9, 2022 Order was entered, she failed to meet the jurisdictional burden under section 1008 of filing the required affidavit with the required elements, showing both (1) "new or different" facts, law, or circumstances warranting reconsideration of the order, and (2) a sufficient explanation for why the "new or different" information was not offered before the order issued. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1008; Pazderka v. Caballeros Dimas Alang, Inc. (1998) 62 Cal. App. 4th 658, 670.) The party moving for reconsideration must not only demonstrate the grounds for reconsideration, but also "show diligence with a satisfactory explanation for not presenting the new or different information earlier." (Even Zohar Constr. & Remodeling, Inc. v. Bellaire Townhouses, LLC (2015) 61 Cal. 4th 830, 833.) The party may not simply resubmit facts, circumstances, or law previously raised and call them "new or different" for the purposes of section 1008. (See, Kerns v. CSE Ins. Grp. (2003) 106 Cal. App. 4th 368, 383.) In any case, Yu's motion lacks substantive merit. Yu has shown no "new or different" facts, law, or circumstances warranting reconsideration. Even if the court were to indulge Yu's inappropriate objections, they, too, lack merit. Yu complains that she did not have sufficient time to make her presentation. Yu could have objected on these grounds at the hearing and did not. Thus, the argument is forfeited. In any event, the record shows that Yu completed her evidentiary presentation before the allocated time ran. Yu complains that her counsel at the hearing did not have an organized and labeled binder of Kmucha's exhibits and this impaired her ability to object. Again, Yu forfeited this argument by not raising it at the hearing, when any issue could have been addressed. In any event, the record shows that Yu's counsel had access to Kmucha's exhibits; indeed, counsel lodged several cogent objections that were, in fact, sustained. Finally, Yu complains about the court's directions regarding the post-hearing supplemental briefs. Specifically, she objects to the court's reference to Probate Code section 6451. Courts commonly direct counsel to brief particular authority after an evidentiary hearing. They do so to avoid surprise. Here, the applicability of section 6451 was a legal, not factual, issue. Yu and Kmucha filed supplemental briefs addressing the issues. And the court's December 9, 2022 Order addressed precisely the issues that were the subject of the order, i.e., whether Jack Genz adopted decedent Lambert Genz and, if so, whether Kmucha has priority for appointment as the representation of decedent's estate. Petitioner Kmucha shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other paterties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 3 of 3]
